title: Header Fields, Representations, Negotiation, Validators, and Caching · Runtime Contract: Response Metadata Order
summary: Clients send repeated and malformed media fields, the server sniffs content, omits Vary, caches personalized responses, and computes validators from unstable JSON ordering. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo response metadata order increment in representation selection and cache contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set Content-Type, Content-Length strategy, Location, validators, Cache-Control, Vary, security fields, and trailers before committing response headers.
Validate request Content-Type and parameters, select an acceptable response representation, and return 415 or 406 with a documented default.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Response Metadata Order · Theory Response Metadata Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2018 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to negotiate a representation and keep validator and privacy decisions independent; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Response Metadata Order contract for the supplied data.
